non_fiction_catalogue.author: Vineet Kumar
non_fiction_catalogue.bio: Vineet Kumar is a scholar warrior, whose adventures span military operations, battle grounds, and beyond. An alumnus of IIM Ahmedabad and Jawaharlal Nehru University, he blends military leadership with deep academic inquiry in defence, peace studies, and geopolitics. From instructing UN peacekeepers across 50+ nations to mastering scuba diving in all the prominent oceans, Vineet turns every experience into a meaningful story and every story into a meaningful experience.

books.title: Untitled Memoir (A Guide to Failure and Love)
books.description: You know the pain: the crushing, cyclical heartbreak that repeats itself no matter how hard you try. Vineet Kumar lived it. A man of immense professional success, he was forced into a crucible of solitude and self- analysis when his personal life shattered for the final time. What he discovered will change how you view every relationship you’ve ever had. Through brutally honest storytelling, this memoir reveals that the root of all failure is not chemistry, but fear. Kumar exposes the 'slight tilt' in our emotional machinery that guarantees we manifest the very abandonment we dread. This is the story of leaving the chaos of external pursuit behind to find inner peace. It is a powerful, spiritual, and intensely practical guide to performing the energetic divorce from the past and executing the radical shift from seeking love out of fear to seeking love out of love. If you’re tired of asking why you keep failing in Love, this is the book that shows you how to stop.

non_fiction_catalogue.author: Dr. Saroj Salelkar
non_fiction_catalogue.bio: Dr. Saroj Salelkar is a physician, visionary author and integrative health thought leader shaping the future of mental resilience. With nearly two decades of experience in occupational and public health, she has been a pioneer as the first lady doctor in Goa’s mining industry and a state-level master trainer for HIV/AIDS. Blending medical science with spirituality and storytelling, her bestselling books draw inspiration from her own transformative journey. An advocate for emotional resilience, she is deeply committed to helping people find their inner warrior and build lasting inner peace. You can connect with her on Instagram, Facebook, YouTube and LinkedIn @drsarojsalelkar or visit her blogs on her website www.drsarojsalelkar.com

books.title: The Arjuna in Me
books.description: The Arjuna in Me by Dr. Saroj Salelkar is a transformative journey from inner despair to awakened strength. Through the story of Bhairavi a modern woman battling depression, self-doubt, and emotional exhaustion the book reinterprets the timeless wisdom of the Bhagavad Gita for today’s complex world. Set on the battlefield of the mind and body, it blends storytelling with practical spiritual insights, revealing how Karma Yoga, self-reflection, and meditation can conquer negative thought patterns and restless emotions. Written in simple, relatable language, The Arjuna in Me empowers readers to rediscover their inner warrior and create a life of purpose, resilience, and lasting inner peace. https://mybook.to/QLIrYT

non_fiction_catalogue.author: Uma Yoganand
non_fiction_catalogue.bio: Uma is a passionate writer who combines her corporate experience with a flair for creative storytelling. With more than 15 years in the corporate sector, including her role as General Manager at a BPO company in Mumbai, she brings a wealth of practical insight to her writing. Uma holds a Master’s degree in Statistics and a Postgraduate Diploma in Journalism, both earned in Bangalore, as well as an Executive MBA from MAHE. Her writing reflects a deep understanding of life’s complexities and is driven by a desire to inspire and empower women and youth to pursue their goals, overcome obstacles, and follow their dreams. Outside of writing, Uma enjoys yoga, hiking, and spending time with her family.

books.title: Untitled (Inspirational Short Stories Portfolio)
books.description: Are you inspired by the lives of ordinary people around you? Read about how they have led extraordinary lives through grit and determination in this uplifting collection of short stories. The book aims to communicate that the road to success is always under construction. It requires consistent effort, adaptability, patience, and unshakeable belief that, no matter the obstacles, there is always a path forward. Have you heard about a Jungian? Follow Anita as she pursues her passion and travels to New York. Read about Sampat, a journalist who seizes the opportunity of a lifetime to create the dream newspaper envisioned by Gopal Krishna Gokhale. These stories of ordinary people will motivate you to live with passion, achieve your dreams, and most importantly, live your life with purpose.

non_fiction_catalogue.author: Chetna Bhatia
non_fiction_catalogue.bio: Ms. Chetna Bhatia, a teacher, an art lover and a nature lover, born and brought up in Jamnagar Gujarat, now settled in Mumbai. She has been in the field of education for around 35 years. Her profession as a teacher developed a passion for writing in her. She has contributed several poems, quotes and stories to more than 50 National and International anthologies and also 2 solo quote books (bestsellers on Amazon) and 2 solo collections of poems. 1 book on short stories. An editor and compiler of 15 Gujarati anthologies, she writes in 3 different languages, English, Hindi and Gujarati and is working towards giving a platform to new writers and keeping the native language (Gujarati) alive.

books.title: Untitled (Love Quotes Gift Book)
books.description: "Love is a feeling that very few people are bestowed with." This book is a compilation of love quotes that can be gifted to your loved ones.

non_fiction_catalogue.author: Vishwa Ratan
non_fiction_catalogue.bio: Dr. Vishwa Ratan is an Eye Specialist as well as a Public Health Specialist. He is wanderlust by nature and has travelled to many countries, working in different hospitals. His previous books include: A Doctor's Journey- A collection of short stories, A Night in the emergency room, and Pandemic stories around the World, are popular among readers.

books.title: Let's Dance in the Rain Again
books.description: "Let's Dance in the Rain Again" is a collection of poems that are expressions of compassion, love, emotions, and desires. For instance, in the "Travelling Bag" poem, one of the deep emotions is when the bag asks its owner, "Why My Master You Are At Home? Have I Done Something Wrong?" The poem tries to depict the pandemic months of helplessness. Likewise, many poems relate to human feelings and closeness to nature.

non_fiction_catalogue.author: Madhuri Maitra
non_fiction_catalogue.bio: Madhuri Maitra's world view is largely informed by her experiences as an army kid and an army wife. She has been a teacher, corporate trainer and writer, and now leads a slower life engaging with family, friends and simple community -enriching activities. She continues to write poetry and non- fiction.

books.title: Haiku and Other Micropoetry
books.description: A short poem is a drop of the divine, a brief, brilliant flash. An emotion surges, you create an image, and the jewel is born, plucked out of thought-flow, rubbed and polished to its innate glow. Haiku is like that. Intrinsically beautiful! Some see the beauty and others do not. Moreover, poetry is a personal experience. There's no saying what will touch the heart, the senses, the brain, and why. Haiku and Other Micropoetry would appeal to many people who have had idyllic childhoods in a world less crowded; who have enjoyed simple pleasures, or not; who have loved, lost, laughed, cried, danced, achieved, failed, and done everything that life bid them. They have searched for meaning and known the pain of that search. They have allowed themselves to grow with every experience. Haiku heals. A short verse brings moments of recognition. A lot more can happen within these pages. Haiku and Other Micropoetry will be read by some with love and some with déjà vu. Some will identify with it, some might just toss it aside, and others might carry it around as a favorite dip-into read.
